name: n8n:conventions
tagline: Quick-reference cheat sheet of n8n coding conventions: TypeScript, Vue frontend, backend DI, testing, and database rules.
verdict: Useful terse reference with good code examples and a sensible pointer to fuller docs, plus a thoughtful secrets-handling note. But the namespaced name breaks kebab-case naming, there is no version/changelog signal, and neither the description nor body says when NOT to use it versus the sibling lint-rule skill.

highlights.summary: Description 'Quick reference for n8n patterns. Full docs /AGENTS.md' says what it is, not concrete trigger situations.
No 'NOT for' guidance despite overlap with adjacent n8n:create-community-node-lint-rule skill.
name: n8n:conventions — contains ':' and fails the kebab-case regex ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$.
